message:
feat(core): asupersync runtime compatibility for spawn_blocking and mpsc channels

All `tokio::task::spawn_blocking` call sites in session_retention,
snapshot_engine, and related modules now use conditional compilation to
dispatch through `asupersync::runtime::spawn_blocking` when the
`asupersync-runtime` feature is active. This ensures ft can run on the
asupersync cooperative runtime without pulling in tokio's blocking
thread pool, which conflicts with asupersync's structured cancellation.

Affected modules and changes:
- session_retention.rs: `cleanup_sessions_async` dual-path spawn_blocking.
- snapshot_engine.rs: Five spawn_blocking call sites (detection loading,
  checkpoint saving, cleanup, session update/create, shutdown marking)
  all wrapped in `#[cfg(feature = "asupersync-runtime")]` blocks.
  Minor formatting adjustments in test assertions (cargo fmt drift).
- runtime_compat.rs: mpsc channel tests now properly use the `Cx`
  parameter for send/recv when asupersync is active, fixing compilation
  under `--features asupersync-runtime`.
- asupersync_cx_infrastructure.rs (integration test): Two new tests —
  `spawn_bounded_helper_clamps_zero_concurrency_to_one` verifies the
  semaphore degrades gracefully when max_concurrency=0, and
  `spawn_bounded_children_observe_parent_cancellation` ensures structured
  cancellation propagates through spawn_bounded_with_cx children.
- Cargo.toml: Added `task_spawn` benchmark target gated behind
  `asupersync-runtime` feature for benchmarking spawn overhead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -120,6 +120,11 @@ name = "cx_propagation"
 harness = false
 required-features = ["asupersync-runtime"]
 
+[[bench]]
+name = "task_spawn"
+harness = false
+required-features = ["asupersync-runtime"]
+
 [[bench]]
 name = "sizing_benchmark"
 harness = false
